title: Revision Selection, Ranges, Search, and Change Discovery · Repository Trace: Revision Names
summary: Answer a focused history question without checking out candidate commits or mistaking range notation for set subtraction in every context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git revision names increment in a revision-query and change-discovery workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve full and abbreviated object IDs, refs, reflog selectors, ancestry suffixes, and peel operators with ambiguity checks.
Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Revision Names
steps.instruction:
theory case — Feature tip F has 4 commits after merge base M while main has two. Compare M..F, main...F, HEAD~2, and path-limited pickaxe results without confusing commit sets with file diffs.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Revision Names while producing a revision-query and change-discovery workbook.
